text:
An Encounter × Chrollo × The Gold Dust Girl (遭遇×クロロ×金粉少女, Sōgū × Kuroro × Kinpun Shōjo) is the 81 st episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 3 rd episode of the Greed Island final OVA series. It was released on DVD on March 3 rd , 2004.

Overview

Killua contacts Kurapika while Hisoka meets up with the Phantom Troupe. Biscuit brings Gon and Killua to different locations to obtain the cards they need to complete the Greed Island.

Synopsis

Killua returns to Greed Island and he and Gon happily reunite. Biscuit shows him all of their newly acquired spell cards, but admits that they haven't tried using any of them aside from Blackout Curtain. Killua suggests that they start learning how to use them and pulls out Contact for them to test first. Gon activates the card, and a list of players they've previously met in-game appears in the book for them to choose from.

Killua's eyes widen when he sees Chrollo's name on the list and asks Gon when he crossed paths with Chrollo in the game. Gon says he never did, and the two get confused as they had thought Chrollo was restricted by Kurapika's Nen ability. Biscuit lashes out at Gon and Killua after they continuously talk over her when she tries to ask questions, but her anger doesn't last long, and she tells them how another person's Nen can be removed from someone via Exorcism. They decide to inform Kurapika of Chrollo's supposed Exorcism, and Killua again exits the game to go tell him.

At the same time, Shalnark also notices Chrollo's name on his list of players and informs the other Spiders with him. Because of the prophecy Chrollo received, Shalnark believes Chrollo came to Greed Island by accident while looking for the Exorcist he sought and was thrown out. He then asked someone else to enter Greed Island under his name to send a message to the other Troupe members. The Spiders change their current course of action and decide to look for the Exorcist Chrollo had been seeking on Greed Island. However, they get interrupted by Hisoka, who comes out of the forest during their planning.

Hisoka corrects Shalnark's explanation by informing them it was actually his own idea to use Chrollo's name, but the Spiders are unimpressed. Hisoka tells them to keep in contact with him if they receive any news of the Exorcist and then leave.

Killua calls Kurapika to tell him the news, but Kurapika says it's impossible for Chrollo to have already removed his Nen since Kurapika would have felt it happen. He thanks Killua for informing him nonetheless and hangs up. Melody comes to retrieve Kurapika and bring him to Light. Neon sits alone in her room, which is a complete mess, and struggles to understand why she can no longer use her Nen ability. Light's room is equally messy, and when Kurapika stands before him, he shakily asks Kurapika what he is supposed to do now. Kurapika tries to tell him he has everything under control, but Light lashes out at him in a panic.

Killua meets back up with Gon and Biscuit, and Biscuit has Gon show Killua how his Nen ability has developed. Killua questions why Gon has to announce his ability before using it since it makes it easier for enemies to anticipate what he will do. However, Gon refuses to drop that part since he loves announcing his ability. However, Biscuit is less worried about it and congratulates them both for getting so far. She decides it is time for them to move on to actually working to clear the game.

They travel to a giant tree where an NPC informs them of the quest to capture King White Stag Beetles that live inside the tree. He tells them to hit the circular mark on the tree as hard as possible and offers them his hammer. Gon declines, however, deciding to use his bare hands. He uses his Rock ability to strike the tree so hard that the entire area around them shakes. Tons of bugs fall from the trees around them, and they can collect three King White Stag Beetles.

They move on to their next task, which involves rescuing a girl from a mansion. The guard outside the estate claims that no such girl exists, but also says only a few people may enter the mansion and that they may trigger traps if they try to sneak in to get to her room. While Gon gets confused while listening to the contradicting instructions, Killua shouts that he has already found her and brings the young girl outside with him. He receives the Gold Dust Girl card as his reward.

The trio then heads to Dorias, where Biscuit says three cards are given out as prizes for an event. On their way, Gon and Killua want to stop by Antokiba to participate in the tournament. They win as a team and receive a Paladin's Necklace as their reward. Not knowing what it is for, they revisit the Antokiba Trade Shop NPC, who explains its usage, and Gon puts the necklace on around his neck. 

In Dorias, they win a Witch's Love Potion card, a Memory Helmet card, and a Risky Dice card. Biscuit also informs them of cards they can get from getting three 7's on the slot machines in the city, but the odds are very low, and it would likely end up costing them a lot of money. Killua rolls the Risky Dice they obtained and manages to get three 7's on the slot machine on his first try. He wins a Rainbow Diamond card. 

Biscuit warns him about getting bad luck when using the dice, and while Killua is trying to convince her that the bad luck the odds are really low. At that moment, an explosion occurs at the casino that injures a player. A patron had said it resulted from the player using some strange dice. Killua still wants to gamble using his Risky Dice; however, he manages to win Fledgling Gambler. 

When he refuses to stop gambling even after that, Biscuit knocks him out, and Gon carries him as they head to the next city.
